name: October
description: Halloween, harvest, and the month that's actually the eighth — but really the tenth.

questions.q: How many days does October have?
From which Latin numerical prefix does October's name derive?

source.notes: Originally the eighth month in the early Roman calendar.

questions.q: Why is October the tenth month rather than the eighth that its name implies?
choices: January and February were added later
An extra leap month was added

source.notes: Added by Numa Pompilius around 700 BCE.

questions.q: What season is October in the Northern Hemisphere?
What are October's two zodiac signs?

source.notes: Libra runs through Oct 22; Scorpio begins Oct 23.

questions.q: What are the traditional birthstones for October?
What is October's birth flower?
On what date is Halloween celebrated each October?

source.notes: From 'All Hallows' Eve' — the night before All Saints' Day (Nov 1).

questions.q: Which ancient Celtic festival, marking the end of harvest, evolved into Halloween?
On what day is Canadian Thanksgiving celebrated?

source.notes: Falls about six weeks before U.S. Thanksgiving.

questions.q: Which U.S. federal holiday traditionally falls on the second Monday of October?

source.notes: Many U.S. states and cities now observe Indigenous Peoples' Day instead, on the same day.

questions.q: Which Major League Baseball event traditionally takes place in October?

source.notes: MLB's championship is played in late October — earning the nickname 'October baseball.'

questions.q: Which 1917 revolution, named for the month in which it took place (per the Julian calendar), brought the Bolsheviks to power in Russia?

source.notes: Took place November 7 by the Gregorian calendar but October 25 by the Julian calendar still in use in Russia.

questions.q: Which 1929 stock-market event began on Black Thursday and ended with Black Tuesday?

source.notes: October 24, 1929 (Black Thursday) and October 29, 1929 (Black Tuesday) precipitated the Great Depression.

questions.q: Which annual German beer festival typically begins in late September and ends in early October?

source.notes: Runs ~16 days in Munich, ending the first Sunday of October.

questions.q: Which 13-day 'crisis' in October 1962 brought the U.S. and Soviet Union to the brink of nuclear war?
On what date is Indigenous Peoples' Day / Columbus Day commemorated worldwide as the day Columbus landed in the Americas?
Awareness ribbons during October most often signify which cause?

source.notes: October is Breast Cancer Awareness Month.

questions.q: What was October's name in the old Anglo-Saxon calendar, related to the season's first full moon?
Which famous 1936 racehorse-rivalry season opener occurred in October — when Seabiscuit's first major win lit up sports pages?

source.notes: Seabiscuit broke through to public attention with the Maryland-area Scarsdale Handicap and others in fall 1936.
